classification: Core Applications / SAP / Report an Issue
symptom_raw: When posting F-53 the reference key becomes HQ instead of JAK even though there is no option for inputting the Reference 1 and 2

reference key.why: User explicitly says 'Reference 1 and 2' in the same sentence — these are the literal labels of XREF1 / XREF2 on BSEG line items

Reference 1 and 2.why: Exact match to SAP field labels

HQ.why: Confirmed in YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1093 as the expected XREF2 value for UNES HQ postings. Stored in USR05 PARVA for PARID='Y_USERFO'. NOT the same 'HQ' or 'UNESCO' as in INC-000006073 (which was an FM fund center).
HQ.anti_confusion_note: INC-000006073 used the string 'UNESCO' for a fund center populated by FMDERIVE exit ZXFMDTU02_RPY. This incident uses the string 'HQ' for an office code populated by forms UXR1/UXR2 in YRGGBS00. Completely different mechanism. Do NOT conflate.

JAK.sap_field: Office code value 'JAK' — Jakarta Field Unit
JAK.why: Matches user's department FU/JAK. Other known field office codes in production: YAO (Yaoundé, CM), KAB (Kabul, AF), DAK (Dakar, SN)

no option for inputting.meaning: F-53 screen does not expose XREF1/XREF2 as editable fields
no option for inputting.technical_impact: BSEG-XREF2 = SPACE at substitution time → UXR2 form enters the USR05 branch (YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1026)

root_cause.description: AL_JONATHAN's USR05 parameter Y_USERFO = 'HQ' instead of 'JAK'. Forms UXR1 (YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:996) and UXR2 (YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1024) read this parameter and unconditionally assign its value to BSEG-XREF1/XREF2 when the fields are blank (always true for F-53). Code works as designed. Defect is master data only.
evidence: Code: YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1000-1004 (UXR1 reads USR05 Y_USERFO and assigns to bseg_xref1)
Code: YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1028-1032 (UXR2 reads USR05 Y_USERFO and assigns to bseg_xref2)
Live RFC read 2026-04-09: USR05 WHERE BNAME='AL_JONATHAN' AND PARID='Y_USERFO' → PARVA='HQ'
Live RFC read 2026-04-09: 10 BSAK line items for AL_JONATHAN's FBZ2 docs in Dec/2025-March/2026 → ALL have XREF1='HQ', XREF2='HQ'
Comparison: field-office users JJ_YAKI-PAHI (YAO), O_RASHIDI (KAB), DA_ENGONGA (DAK) correctly have their office codes — mechanism works for them

user_posting_history_window.source: Gold DB bkpf + live RFC BSAK

tactical: 3. Smoke test with a test F-53 posting
strategic: 1. Audit all UNES users with Y_USERFO='HQ' vs HR master (PA0001.PERSA/ORGEH) to find more mis-configured field users
2. Add periodic validation job Y_USERFO vs HR master
3. Re-enable the commented-out IF bseg_xref1 = space guard in UXR1 (line 998)
4. Consider replacing SU3-parameter lookup with HR-master lookup
questions_for_business: Is Anthony's correct office code 'JAK' or a more granular value (JKT / JAKA / IDN)?
Should the fix cover only AL_JONATHAN or the full class of mis-configured users?

known_unknowns_opened: KU-028: how many UNES users have Y_USERFO='HQ' but HR master says field-unit?
KU-029: downstream impact of wrong XREF2 on AL_JONATHAN's 14 docs (F110/BCM routing)?
KU-030: was UXR1's IF bseg_xref1 = space guard intentionally commented out, when, and why?

prior_wrong_path_session.attempted_mechanism: FMDERIVE exit ZXFMDTU02_RPY hardcoding FUND_CENTER='UNESCO' for HKONT ∈ (0006045011, 0007045011, 0006045014)
prior_wrong_path_session.disposition: Wrong mechanism — FMDERIVE populates FICTR/FUND, not BSEG.XREF1/XREF2. May describe a real separate defect worth tracking as an observation. See §13 of canonical report.
